        - name: jobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201': { description: Appeal created }

  /jobs/{jobId}/appeals/{appealId}/decide:
    post:
      tags: [Jobs]
      summary: Decide appeal
      description: Requires `JOB_APPROVE` and step-up action `JOB_APPEAL_DECIDE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: jobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: appealId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            example:
              decision: APPROVE
              comment: Reinstated after review
      responses:
        '200': { description: Appeal decision recorded }

  /jobs/{jobId}/history:
    get:
      tags: [Jobs]
      summary: Job version/workflow history
      description: Requires `JOB_READ`; non-admin employers are owner-scoped.
      parameters:
        - name: jobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Job history payload }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }

  /analytics/metrics:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create metric definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_METRIC_MANAGE` (Administrator).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Metric created }

  /analytics/dimensions:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create dimension definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_DIMENSION_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Dimension created }

  /analytics/anomaly-rules:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create anomaly rule
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_METRIC_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Rule created }

  /analytics/dashboards:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create dashboard definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Dashboard created }

  /analytics/dashboards/{dashboardId}:
    get:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Get dashboard values and anomaly evaluations
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_READ`.
      parameters:
        - name: dashboardId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Dashboard payload }

  /analytics/reports:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create report definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Report definition created }

  /analytics/reports/{reportId}/run:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Trigger report run immediately
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: reportId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Report run result }
        '500':
          description: Run failed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }

  /analytics/reports/{reportId}/runs:
    get:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: List report runs
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_READ` (Administrator, Auditor).
      parameters:
        - name: reportId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Report runs list }

  /exports:
    post:
      tags: [Exports]
      summary: Queue export job
      description: Requires `EXPORT_CREATE` and step-up action `EXPORT_CREATE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            example:
              resource: participants
              format: CSV
              filters: {}
              fields: [name, phone, email, notes]
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Job queued
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  exportJobId: exp_9fd3a8e2
                  status: QUEUED

  /exports/{exportJobId}:
    get:
      tags: [Exports]
      summary: Export job status/details
      description: |
        Requires `EXPORT_READ` and object-level authorization.
        Only requester or Administrator can access the job.
      parameters:
        - name: exportJobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Export job details }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /admin/config:
    get:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Read runtime config summary
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      responses:
        '200': { description: Config summary }
    patch:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Update runtime config (not implemented)
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE` and step-up action `ADMIN_CONFIG_UPDATE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      responses:
        '501':
          description: Not implemented
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }

  /admin/cache/invalidate:
    post:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Invalidate cache scopes
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '200': { description: Invalidation result }

  /admin/reconciliation/artifacts:
    get:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: List report/export artifacts and checksums
      description: Requires `RECONCILIATION_READ`.
      responses:
        '200': { description: Artifact list }

  /audit/events:
    get:
      tags: [Audit]
      summary: Query immutable audit events
      description: Requires `AUDIT_READ` (Administrator, Auditor).
      parameters:
        - name: page
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1 }
        - name: pageSize
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 50 }
        - name: filter[action]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[actorId]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[entityType]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[from]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, format: date-time }
        - name: filter[to]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, format: date-time }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Event list (metadata sanitized) }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

components:
  securitySchemes:
    SessionCookie:
      type: apiKey
      in: cookie
      name: museum_sid
      description: Session cookie set by `POST /auth/login`.
    CsrfHeader:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-CSRF-Token
      description: Required on mutating requests except `POST /auth/login`.
    StepUpHeader:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-Step-Up-Token
      description: One-time action-bound step-up token for sensitive operations.
  parameters:
    UserId:
      name: userId
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    DraftId:
      name: draftId
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
  schemas:
    Role:
      type: string
      enum:
        - Administrator
        - Curator
        - Exhibit Manager
        - Program Coordinator
        - Employer
        - Reviewer
        - Auditor
    LoginResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        data:
          type: object
          properties:
            user:
              type: object
              properties:
                id: { type: string }
                username: { type: string }
                roles:
                  type: array
                  items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Role' }
            session:
              type: object
              properties:
                id: { type: string }
                idleExpiresAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
            csrfToken: { type: string }
    ErrorEnvelope:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: object
          required: [code, message, requestId]
          properties:
            code: { type: string }
            message: { type: string }
            details:
              oneOf:
                - type: array
                  items: { type: object }
                - type: object
            requestId: { type: string }
  responses:
    ValidationError:
      description: Validation failed
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    AuthError:
      description: Invalid credentials or account lock
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Unauthenticated:
      description: Authentication required or session expired
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Forbidden:
      description: Insufficient role permission or object-level access denied
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    NotFound:
      description: Resource not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Conflict:
      description: State conflict
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    GraphValidationBlocked:
      description: Blocking graph validation issues remain
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
files.after:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Philatelic Museum Operations Suite API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: |
    Public HTTP API for museum operations.

    Authentication model:
    - Session cookie `museum_sid` (set on `POST /auth/login`)
    - `X-CSRF-Token` required on mutating requests (`POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE`) except `POST /auth/login`
    - `X-Step-Up-Token` required on sensitive actions after `POST /auth/step-up`

    Step-up actions:
    - `GRAPH_PUBLISH`
    - `ROUTE_RULE_CHANGE`
    - `JOB_APPROVE`
    - `JOB_APPEAL_DECIDE`
    - `EXPORT_CREATE`
    - `ADMIN_CONFIG_UPDATE`
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:8080/api/v1
tags:
  - name: Health
  - name: Auth
  - name: Users
  - name: Catalog
  - name: Graph
  - name: Venues & Routes
  - name: Programs
  - name: Inbox
  - name: Jobs
  - name: Analytics
  - name: Exports
  - name: Admin
  - name: Audit
security:
  - SessionCookie: []
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      tags: [Health]
      summary: Service and database health
      security: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Health payload
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  data:
                    type: object
              example:
                data:
                  status: ok
                  service: backend
                  sessionCookie:
                    name: museum_sid
                    httpOnly: true
                    secure: false
                    sameSite: strict
                    idleTtlSeconds: 1800
                    absoluteTtlSeconds: 43200
                  db:
                    ready: true
                    connected: true
                    readyState: 1
                    lastError: null

  /auth/login:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Login and create authenticated session
      description: Bootstrap endpoint exempt from CSRF header requirement.
      security: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [username, password]
              properties:
                username: { type: string }
                password: { type: string }
            example:
              username: curator.dev
              password: CuratorSecure!2026
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Authenticated
          headers:
            Set-Cookie:
              schema:
                type: string
              description: Session cookie `museum_sid`
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LoginResponse'
              example:
                data:
                  user:
                    id: 65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06a001
                    username: curator.dev
                    roles: [Curator]
                  session:
                    id: x9pR2lD_session
                    idleExpiresAt: '2026-03-30T10:00:00.000Z'
                  csrfToken: a7815c8d-2f15-42f1-b8a9-a4ef880d3123
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/AuthError'

  /auth/logout:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Logout and destroy session
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '204': { description: Logged out }

  /auth/me:
    get:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Current session principal and session metadata
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Current principal
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthenticated'

  /auth/step-up:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Verify credentials and issue one-time action-bound step-up token
      description: Requires authenticated session and CSRF token.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [password, action]
              properties:
                password: { type: string }
                action:
                  type: string
                  enum: [GRAPH_PUBLISH, ROUTE_RULE_CHANGE, JOB_APPROVE, JOB_APPEAL_DECIDE, EXPORT_CREATE, ADMIN_CONFIG_UPDATE]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Step-up token issued
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  stepUpToken: stp_2fbf4e9f2e5a4d8faea6
                  action: GRAPH_PUBLISH
                  validUntil: '2026-03-30T10:10:00.000Z'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/AuthError'

  /users:
    get:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: List users (excluding password hashes)
      description: Requires permission `USERS_ADMIN` (Administrator).
      responses:
        '200': { description: User list }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
    post:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Create user
      description: Requires permission `USERS_ADMIN` (Administrator).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [username, password, roles]
              properties:
                username: { type: string }
                password: { type: string }
                roles:
                  type: array
                  items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Role' }
      responses:
        '201': { description: User created }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }
        '409': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict' }

  /users/{userId}:
    patch:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Update user roles/status
      description: Requires permission `USERS_ADMIN` (Administrator).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/UserId'
      responses:
        '200': { description: User updated }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /catalog/search:
    get:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Catalog search
      description: Publicly accessible search with caching and fuzzy relevance.
      security: []
      parameters:
        - name: q
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: page
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, default: 1 }
        - name: pageSize
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 50, default: 20 }
        - name: sort
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [relevance:desc, title:asc, period:asc]
        - name: filter[category]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[tags]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[periodId]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[seriesId]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Search results
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  - id: 65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06b111
                    title: Blue Airmail
                    catalogNumber: CAT-123
                    artist: I. Kline
                    series: Airmail
                    country: USA
                    period: 1930s
                    tags: [airmail, blue]
                pagination:
                  page: 1
                  pageSize: 20
                  total: 1
                  totalPages: 1
                meta:
                  cache: MISS
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /catalog/autocomplete:
    get:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Autocomplete suggestions
      security: []
      parameters:
        - name: q
          in: query
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, minLength: 1, maxLength: 64 }
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 15, default: 8 }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Suggestions }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /catalog/hot-keywords:
    get:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Active curated hot keywords
      security: []
      responses:
        '200': { description: Keywords }
    post:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Create hot keyword
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION` (Administrator, Curator).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Created }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }

  /catalog/hot-keywords/{keywordId}:
    patch:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Update hot keyword
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: keywordId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Updated }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
    delete:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Retire hot keyword
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: keywordId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '204': { description: Retired }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /catalog/items:
    post:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Create catalog item
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Created }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /catalog/items/{itemId}:
    patch:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Update catalog item
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: itemId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Updated }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
    delete:
      tags: [Catalog]
      summary: Archive catalog item
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: itemId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '204': { description: Archived }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /graph/versions:
    get:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: List published graph versions
      description: Requires `GRAPH_READ`.
      responses:
        '200': { description: Versions list }

  /graph/drafts:
    post:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Create graph draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT` (Administrator, Curator).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Draft created }

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}:
    get:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Read draft snapshot and validation report
      description: |
        Requires `GRAPH_READ`. Unpublished drafts are restricted to Administrator/Curator or draft owner.
      parameters:
        - name: draftId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Draft payload }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}/nodes:
    post:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Add node to draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
      responses:
        '201': { description: Node created }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}/nodes/{nodeId}:
    patch:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Update node in draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
        - name: nodeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Node updated }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
    delete:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Delete node in draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
        - name: nodeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '204': { description: Node deleted }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}/edges:
    post:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Add edge to draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
      responses:
        '201': { description: Edge created }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}/validate:
    post:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Run draft validation
      description: Requires `GRAPH_DRAFT_EDIT`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Validation report
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  status: INVALID
                  issues:
                    - code: CONSTRAINT_ALLOWED_TARGET_TYPES
                      severity: BLOCKING
                      edgeId: e_a1b2c3d4
                      message: Edge target type ARTIST is not allowed for relation CREATED_BY

  /graph/drafts/{draftId}/publish:
    post:
      tags: [Graph]
      summary: Publish graph draft
      description: Requires `GRAPH_PUBLISH` and `X-Step-Up-Token` for action `GRAPH_PUBLISH`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/DraftId'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Published
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  version: 7
                  publishedAt: '2026-03-30T10:15:00.000Z'
        '422':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/GraphValidationBlocked'

  /venues:
    post:
      tags: [Venues & Routes]
      summary: Create venue
      description: Requires `VENUE_MANAGE` (Administrator, Exhibit Manager).
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Venue created }

  /venues/{venueId}/halls:
    post:
      tags: [Venues & Routes]
      summary: Create hall
      description: Requires `VENUE_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: venueId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201': { description: Hall created }

  /halls/{hallId}/zones:

      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: hallId
          in: path
          required: true

      responses:

  /zones/{zoneId}/display-cases:

          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201': { description: Display case created }

  /routes:
    post:
      tags: [Venues & Routes]
      summary: Create route definition
      description: Requires `VENUE_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Route created }

  /routes/{routeId}/segments:
    post:

      description: Requires `VENUE_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []

          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:

        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

  /routes/{routeId}:
    patch:

      description: Requires `ROUTE_RULE_CHANGE` and step-up action `ROUTE_RULE_CHANGE`.
      security:

      parameters:

      responses:
        '200': { description: Route updated }

  /routes/{routeId}/itineraries:
    post:
      tags: [Venues & Routes]
      summary: Generate itinerary from route segments
      description: Requires `VENUE_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: routeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            example:
              accessibilityMode: true
              branchSelections:
                - fromCaseId: 65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06c101
                  toCaseId: 65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06c102
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Itinerary generated
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  itineraryId: iti_8a6c9e11
                  estimatedWalkMinutes: 7.1
                  printable:
                    routeId: rte_10f7ab22
                    accessibilityMode: true
                    steps:
                      - step: 1
                        segmentType: REQUIRED_NEXT
                        fromCaseId: '65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06c101'
                        toCaseId: '65f0f7d1d5f5f8612f06c102'
                        dwellMinutes: 4
                        distanceMeters: 40

  /programs:
    post:
      tags: [Programs]
      summary: Create program

  /coaches:
    post:

  /coaches/{coachId}/availability:
    post:

  /program-sessions:
    post:
      tags: [Programs]
      summary: Create program session
      description: Requires `PROGRAM_MANAGE`; enforces coach availability window.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []

  /program-sessions/{sessionId}/registrations:
    post:

  /program-sessions/{sessionId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel:

  /program-sessions/{sessionId}/registrations/{registrationId}/no-show:

          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }

  /program-sessions/{sessionId}/waitlist/{entryId}/confirm:

      tags: [Programs]

      description: Requires `PROGRAM_MANAGE`.

  /participants/{participantId}/credits/adjustments:

  /inbox/messages:

  /jobs/{jobId}/submit:
    post:

        - SessionCookie: []

  /jobs/{jobId}/approve:

  /jobs/{jobId}/takedown:

  /jobs/{jobId}/appeals:

          CsrfHeader: []

          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201': { description: Appeal created }

  /jobs/{jobId}/appeals/{appealId}/decide:
    post:
      tags: [Jobs]
      summary: Decide appeal
      description: Requires `JOB_APPROVE` and step-up action `JOB_APPEAL_DECIDE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      parameters:

          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }

        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            example:
              decision: APPROVE
              comment: Reinstated after review
      responses:
        '200': { description: Appeal decision recorded }

  /jobs/{jobId}/history:
    get:
      tags: [Jobs]
      summary: Job version/workflow history

          in: path
          required: true

  /analytics/metrics:

  /analytics/dimensions:

      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:

  /analytics/anomaly-rules:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create anomaly rule
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_METRIC_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Rule created }

  /analytics/dashboards:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create dashboard definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Dashboard created }

  /analytics/dashboards/{dashboardId}:
    get:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Get dashboard values and anomaly evaluations

      parameters:
        - name: dashboardId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Dashboard payload }

  /analytics/reports:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Create report definition
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:
        '201': { description: Report definition created }

  /analytics/reports/{reportId}/run:
    post:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: Trigger report run immediately
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      parameters:
        - name: reportId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Report run result }
        '500':
          description: Run failed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }

  /analytics/reports/{reportId}/runs:
    get:
      tags: [Analytics]
      summary: List report runs
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_READ` (Administrator, Auditor).
      parameters:
        - name: reportId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Report runs list }

  /exports:
    post:
      tags: [Exports]
      summary: Queue export job
      description: Requires `EXPORT_CREATE` and step-up action `EXPORT_CREATE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            example:
              resource: participants
              format: CSV
              filters: {}
              fields: [name, phone, email, notes]
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Job queued
          content:
            application/json:
              example:
                data:
                  exportJobId: exp_9fd3a8e2
                  status: QUEUED

  /exports/{exportJobId}:
    get:
      tags: [Exports]
      summary: Export job status/details
      description: |
        Requires `EXPORT_READ` and object-level authorization.
        Only requester or Administrator can access the job.
      parameters:
        - name: exportJobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Export job details }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }

  /admin/config:
    get:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Read runtime config summary
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE`.
      responses:
        '200': { description: Config summary }
    patch:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Update runtime config (not implemented)
      description: Requires `ANALYTICS_REPORT_MANAGE` and step-up action `ADMIN_CONFIG_UPDATE`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
          StepUpHeader: []
      responses:
        '501':
          description: Not implemented
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }

  /admin/cache/invalidate:
    post:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: Invalidate cache scopes
      description: Requires `CATALOG_CURATION`.
      security:
        - SessionCookie: []
          CsrfHeader: []
      responses:

  /admin/reconciliation/artifacts:
    get:
      tags: [Admin]
      summary: List report/export artifacts and checksums
      description: Requires `RECONCILIATION_READ`.
      responses:
        '200': { description: Artifact list }

  /audit/events:
    get:
      tags: [Audit]
      summary: Query immutable audit events
      description: Requires `AUDIT_READ` (Administrator, Auditor).
      parameters:
        - name: page
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1 }
        - name: pageSize
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 50 }
        - name: filter[action]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[actorId]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[entityType]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: filter[from]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, format: date-time }
        - name: filter[to]
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, format: date-time }
      responses:
        '200': { description: Event list (metadata sanitized) }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ValidationError' }

components:
  securitySchemes:
    SessionCookie:
      type: apiKey
      in: cookie
      name: museum_sid
      description: Session cookie set by `POST /auth/login`.
    CsrfHeader:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-CSRF-Token
      description: Required on mutating requests except `POST /auth/login`.
    StepUpHeader:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-Step-Up-Token
      description: One-time action-bound step-up token for sensitive operations.
  parameters:
    UserId:
      name: userId
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    DraftId:
      name: draftId
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
  schemas:
    Role:
      type: string
      enum:
        - Administrator
        - Curator
        - Exhibit Manager
        - Program Coordinator
        - Employer
        - Reviewer
        - Auditor
    LoginResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        data:
          type: object
          properties:
            user:
              type: object
              properties:
                id: { type: string }
                username: { type: string }
                roles:
                  type: array
                  items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Role' }
            session:
              type: object
              properties:
                id: { type: string }
                idleExpiresAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
            csrfToken: { type: string }
    ErrorEnvelope:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: object
          required: [code, message, requestId]
          properties:
            code: { type: string }
            message: { type: string }
            details:
              oneOf:
                - type: array
                  items: { type: object }
                - type: object
            requestId: { type: string }
  responses:
    ValidationError:
      description: Validation failed
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    AuthError:
      description: Invalid credentials or account lock
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Unauthenticated:
      description: Authentication required or session expired
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Forbidden:
      description: Insufficient role permission or object-level access denied
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    NotFound:
      description: Resource not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    Conflict:
      description: State conflict
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }
    GraphValidationBlocked:
      description: Blocking graph validation issues remain
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorEnvelope' }

files.before:
# Philatelic Museum Operations Suite

Full-stack operations platform for a philatelic museum: collection discovery, knowledge graph curation, exhibit route orchestration, program operations, staffing governance, analytics/reporting, secure exports, and auditability.

## 1) Local-first Quickstart (Primary Path)

External prerequisite: **MongoDB is the only required external service**.

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+ and npm
- MongoDB reachable at `localhost:27017`
- MongoDB credentials expected by defaults: `museum_user` / `museum_pass`

### Verify MongoDB first

```bash
node -e "const mongoose=require('./backend/node_modules/mongoose');mongoose.connect('mongodb://museum_user:museum_pass@localhost:27017/museum_ops?authSource=admin',{serverSelectionTimeoutMS:5000}).then(()=>{console.log('mongo-ok');process.exit(0)}).catch((e)=>{console.error('mongo-fail',e.message);process.exit(1)})"
```

Expected output:

- `mongo-ok`

### Install dependencies

```bash
cd backend && npm install
cd ../frontend && npm install
cd ..
```

Alternative from repository root:

```bash
npm install --prefix backend
npm install --prefix frontend
```

### Configure environment

```bash
cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env
```

Defaults in those files are local-safe and runnable without source edits.

### Run backend

```bash
cd backend && npm run start
```

Expected output includes:

- `Backend listening on port 8080`
- `Report scheduler started`

### Seed development users (run in another shell)

macOS/Linux:

```bash
cd backend && ENABLE_DEV_SEED=true npm run seed:dev
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
cd backend
$env:ENABLE_DEV_SEED='true'
npm run seed:dev
```

### Run frontend

```bash
cd frontend && npm run dev
```

Frontend URL: `http://localhost:5173`

Backend API base: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1`

### OpenAPI / Swagger UI

- With local `npm --prefix backend run start` (non-production), Swagger UI is available at `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/docs`.
- In Docker (`NODE_ENV=production`), docs are shown only when `ENABLE_SWAGGER=true` (default in `docker-compose.yml`). Set `ENABLE_SWAGGER=false` to hide docs.
- Raw OpenAPI file is served at `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/docs/openapi.yaml`.
- Postman and Swagger use the same base URL: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1`.
- `FRONTEND_ORIGIN` must include `http://localhost:8080` when using Swagger on the API host; `.env.example` and `docker-compose.yml` now include both `http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:8080`.
- Auth notes for both tools:
  - Login first via `POST /auth/login` to obtain cookie `museum_sid`.
  - Send `X-CSRF-Token` on mutating requests (login is exempt).
  - Send `X-Step-Up-Token` for sensitive actions after `POST /auth/step-up`.
- Safety note: enabling Swagger in production exposes API documentation; keep `ENABLE_SWAGGER=false` in real production deployments.

## 2) Runtime Readiness Behavior

- Health endpoint is always reachable at `GET /api/v1/health`.
- Health includes DB state (`data.db.ready`, `data.db.connected`, `data.db.lastError`).
- If DB is not ready, DB-backed endpoints fail fast with:
  - HTTP `503`
  - code `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`
  - clear retry message/details
- Report scheduler does not crash process when DB is unavailable.
- CSRF behavior: `POST /api/v1/auth/login` is bootstrap-exempt; unsafe routes after login still require `X-CSRF-Token`.
- Program scheduling behavior: `POST /api/v1/program-sessions` now enforces coach availability windows and returns `422 COACH_UNAVAILABLE` when outside declared windows.
- Lockout behavior: failed login attempts for existing users count toward lockout even when submitted password format is weak.
- Structured runtime logs include `requestId`, `route`, `actorId`, `action`, `outcome`, and `errorCode` (without request body/password logging).
- Export detail access is object-scoped: requester or Administrator only.
- Catalog pagination contract: `pageSize` accepts up to **51**; `52+` returns validation error.

## 3) Deterministic Verification Commands

### Fast local smoke check

```bash
bash scripts/smoke-local.sh
```

Expected output:

- `[smoke] PASS: health ok, db ready, auth login ok, catalog search ok`

This command returns non-zero with actionable hints if a check fails.

### Full tests

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

If MongoDB is unreachable, the script now fails fast with an actionable preflight message and a copy-paste connectivity check command.

Expected summary:

- `unit_tests : PASS`
- `API_tests  : PASS`
- `Overall    : PASS`

Coverage highlights in the existing suite now include:

- coach availability window enforcement (`in-window` success, `out-of-window` rejection)
- lockout accounting when repeated weak-format login attempts target an existing user
- route segment payload guards (invalid dwell/distance) and invalid optional branch selection rejection
- session idle-expiry boundary unit check without wall-clock waiting
- step-up token expiry timeout enforcement
- audit mutation attempts blocked and invalid audit date filters rejected
- catalog `pageSize` boundary contract (`51` allowed, `52` rejected)

### Frontend verification (cross-platform)

Run these commands from the repository root (`npm --prefix ...` works on Windows/macOS/Linux shells):

1) Install frontend dependencies

```bash
npm --prefix frontend install
```

Expected output includes:

- `added ... packages` (first run) or `up to date` (subsequent runs)
- `found 0 vulnerabilities` (or npm audit summary)

2) Build frontend production bundle

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run build
```

Expected output includes:

- `vite build`
- `built in ...`

3) Run unit/component/integration suites separately

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:unit
npm --prefix frontend run test:component
npm --prefix frontend run test:integration
```

Expected output includes:

- `ok` summary for node unit tests
- `Test Files ... passed` for vitest component/integration runs

4) Run all frontend tests in one command

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:frontend
```

Expected output includes all three suites passing in sequence.

5) Install Playwright browser prerequisite (required once per machine/CI image)

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e:setup
```

This runs `playwright install chromium` and output includes browser download/install completion (or already-installed confirmation).

6) Run E2E suite

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e
```

Expected output includes Playwright passing summary for all specs.

Frontend verification coverage includes:

- blank login form defaults unless explicitly enabled for non-production development
- offline queue sanitization (no token/header persistence)
- user-scoped API cache behavior and logout/user-switch purge hooks
- role/tab access checks (allowed + forbidden)
- search loading/empty/error/sort/pagination behavior
- restricted panel render guard denial behavior
- route-builder validation and itinerary generation flow states
- program/staffing failure and retry behavior

Playwright scenarios now verify:

- user A -> logout -> user B cross-user isolation
- restricted panel denial on unauthorized hash access attempts
- happy paths for search, graph publish, route itinerary generation, program scheduling, and staffing approval

## 4) Prompt Capability Mapping (Theme Fidelity)

The implementation maps directly to the business prompt scope:

- **Collection discovery/search**: catalog search, fuzzy matching, autocomplete, hot keywords, curation CRUD, and UI controls for category/tags/period/series filters with `pageSize <= 51`.
- **Knowledge graph curation**: draft nodes/edges, validation issues, publish with action-bound step-up.
- **Exhibit hierarchy + routes**: venue -> hall -> zone -> display case hierarchy plus visual Route Builder canvas with typed route edges (`REQUIRED_NEXT`, `OPTIONAL_BRANCH`, `ACCESSIBILITY_DETOUR`), and guided read-only navigation/itinerary consumption for ROUTE_READ roles.
- **Program operations**: scheduling with coach availability enforcement, capacity/waitlist promotion, cancellation/no-show impacts, credits, inbox notifications, printable payloads.
- **Staffing governance**: job draft/submit/approve/reject/takedown/appeal/decision workflow with ownership checks.
- **Auth and controls**: password policy, lockout accounting on repeated failures (including weak-format attempts for existing users), session security, RBAC, one-time action-bound step-up, immutable audit events.
- **Analytics/reporting**: metrics, dashboards, anomaly rules with dedupe, report runs, reconciliation artifacts with checksum sidecars.
- **Exports and masking**: permissioned exports with sensitive-field masking policies and object-level read guard on export job details.
- **Offline-ready UX**: app-shell service-worker caching + write queueing on true network failures + sync controls (API reads are network-only to avoid private cache leakage).

## 5) Scope & Non-goals

- This project is a working implementation of the museum operations prompt, not a mock-only shell.
- Some UI surfaces prioritize operational clarity over advanced visual polish.
- Current visual Route Builder uses an SVG graph editor (click-to-link + typed segment editor) rather than a third-party diagram framework; this still satisfies the required visual orchestration behavior.
- No external SaaS dependencies are required for local verification beyond MongoDB.

## 6) Frontend Visual Route Builder Verification

1) Sign in as `admin.dev` (or a role with route permissions) in the Operations Console.
2) Open **Route Builder** and create venue/hall/zone/route.
3) Add display cases and confirm they appear as nodes on the canvas and in the hierarchy tree.
4) Link nodes and assign segment type/dwell/distance; confirm typed directional edges and order labels render on canvas.
5) Commit segments and generate itinerary; confirm itinerary ordering reflects the visual route.

## 6.1) Guided Navigation Verification (ROUTE_READ)

1) Sign in as a non-manager read role (for example `reviewer.dev`).
2) Open **Guided Navigation** tab and enter a known route id.
3) Confirm route metadata and segment list load via read-only endpoints.
4) Confirm printable itinerary payloads are visible when itineraries exist.

## 7) Seeded Development Users

- `admin.dev` / `AdminSecure!2026`
- `curator.dev` / `CuratorSecure!2026`
- `reviewer.dev` / `ReviewerSecure!2026`
- `coordinator.dev` / `CoordinatorSecure!2026`
- `employer.dev` / `EmployerSecure!2026`
- `auditor.dev` / `AuditorSecure!2026`

## 8) Clean delivery packaging note

For source-of-truth delivery snapshots, exclude generated/vendor artifacts:

- `**/node_modules/`
- `frontend/dist/`

These are intentionally ignored in `.gitignore` and should not be included in submission bundles.

Verify clean packaging before delivery:

```bash
bash scripts/verify-clean-delivery.sh
```

## 9) Acceptance Evidence

| Command | Expected result | Criterion proved |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `node -e "...mongoose.connect..."` | prints `mongo-ok` | 1.1.1 external prerequisite is explicit/verifiable |
| `npm --prefix backend run start` then `curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health` | health returns `data.status` + `data.db.ready` | 1.1.1 runtime starts and health is inspectable |
| `bash scripts/smoke-local.sh` | prints `[smoke] PASS: health ok, db ready, auth login ok, catalog search ok` | 1.1.2 deterministic local verification |
| `./run_tests.sh` | summary shows `Overall : PASS` | 1.1.3 reproducible test-backed verification |
| Open Route Builder UI and follow section 6 | visual node/edge orchestration for typed segments | 1.3.1 prompt theme fidelity (visual route orchestration) |
| Review section 4 capability mapping | end-to-end feature map aligns with museum prompt | 1.3.2 no severe scope/theme deviation |

## 10) Acceptance Remediation

- Fixed pagination contract mismatch to prompt requirement (`pageSize <= 51`) across backend validation, API tests, and UI hint text.
- Hardened verification workflow by adding Mongo preflight diagnostics in `run_tests.sh` so environment failures are explicit and actionable.
- Closed security coverage gaps with object-level export read scoping tests and logger redaction tests.
- Added focused prompt-nuance coverage (12-hour late-cancel boundary, audit date filter validation, step-up expiry timeout, itinerary formula assertion).

files.before:
# Philatelic Museum Operations Suite

Full-stack operations platform for a philatelic museum: collection discovery, knowledge graph curation, exhibit route orchestration, program operations, staffing governance, analytics/reporting, secure exports, and auditability.

## 1) Local-first Quickstart (Primary Path)

External prerequisite: **MongoDB is the only required external service**.

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+ and npm
- MongoDB reachable at `localhost:27017`
- MongoDB credentials expected by defaults: `museum_user` / `museum_pass`

### Verify MongoDB first

```bash
node -e "const mongoose=require('./backend/node_modules/mongoose');mongoose.connect('mongodb://museum_user:museum_pass@localhost:27017/museum_ops?authSource=admin',{serverSelectionTimeoutMS:5000}).then(()=>{console.log('mongo-ok');process.exit(0)}).catch((e)=>{console.error('mongo-fail',e.message);process.exit(1)})"
```

Expected output:

- `mongo-ok`

### Install dependencies

```bash
cd backend && npm install
cd ../frontend && npm install
cd ..
```

Alternative from repository root:

```bash
npm install --prefix backend
npm install --prefix frontend
```

### Configure environment

```bash
cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
cp frontend/.env.example frontend/.env
```

Defaults in those files are local-safe and runnable without source edits.

### Run backend

```bash
cd backend && npm run start
```

Expected output includes:

- `Backend listening on port 8080`
- `Report scheduler started`

### Seed development users (run in another shell)

macOS/Linux:

```bash
cd backend && ENABLE_DEV_SEED=true npm run seed:dev
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
cd backend
$env:ENABLE_DEV_SEED='true'
npm run seed:dev
```

### Run frontend

```bash
cd frontend && npm run dev
```

Frontend URL: `http://localhost:5173`

Backend API base: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1`

### OpenAPI / Swagger UI

- With local `npm --prefix backend run start` (non-production), Swagger UI is available at `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/docs`.
- In Docker (`NODE_ENV=production`), docs are shown only when `ENABLE_SWAGGER=true` (default in `docker-compose.yml`). Set `ENABLE_SWAGGER=false` to hide docs.
- Raw OpenAPI file is served at `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/docs/openapi.yaml`.
- Postman and Swagger use the same base URL: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1`.
- `FRONTEND_ORIGIN` must include `http://localhost:8080` when using Swagger on the API host; `.env.example` and `docker-compose.yml` now include both `http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:8080`.
- Auth notes for both tools:
  - Login first via `POST /auth/login` to obtain cookie `museum_sid`.
  - Send `X-CSRF-Token` on mutating requests (login is exempt).
  - Send `X-Step-Up-Token` for sensitive actions after `POST /auth/step-up`.
- Safety note: enabling Swagger in production exposes API documentation; keep `ENABLE_SWAGGER=false` in real production deployments.

## 2) Runtime Readiness Behavior

- Health endpoint is always reachable at `GET /api/v1/health`.
- Health includes DB state (`data.db.ready`, `data.db.connected`, `data.db.lastError`).
- If DB is not ready, DB-backed endpoints fail fast with:
  - HTTP `503`
  - code `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`
  - clear retry message/details
- Report scheduler does not crash process when DB is unavailable.
- CSRF behavior: `POST /api/v1/auth/login` is bootstrap-exempt; unsafe routes after login still require `X-CSRF-Token`.
- Program scheduling behavior: `POST /api/v1/program-sessions` now enforces coach availability windows and returns `422 COACH_UNAVAILABLE` when outside declared windows.
- Lockout behavior: failed login attempts for existing users count toward lockout even when submitted password format is weak.
- Structured runtime logs include `requestId`, `route`, `actorId`, `action`, `outcome`, and `errorCode` (without request body/password logging).
- Export detail access is object-scoped: requester or Administrator only.
- Catalog pagination contract: `pageSize` accepts up to **50**; `51+` returns validation error.

## 3) Deterministic Verification Commands

### Fast local smoke check

```bash
bash scripts/smoke-local.sh
```

Expected output:

- `[smoke] PASS: health ok, db ready, auth login ok, catalog search ok`

This command returns non-zero with actionable hints if a check fails.

### Full tests

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

If MongoDB is unreachable, the script now fails fast with an actionable preflight message and a copy-paste connectivity check command.

Expected summary:

- `unit_tests : PASS`
- `API_tests  : PASS`
- `Overall    : PASS`

Coverage highlights in the existing suite now include:

- coach availability window enforcement (`in-window` success, `out-of-window` rejection)
- lockout accounting when repeated weak-format login attempts target an existing user
- route segment payload guards (invalid dwell/distance) and invalid optional branch selection rejection
- session idle-expiry boundary unit check without wall-clock waiting
- step-up token expiry timeout enforcement
- audit mutation attempts blocked and invalid audit date filters rejected
- catalog `pageSize` boundary contract (`50` allowed, `51` rejected)

### Frontend verification (cross-platform)

Run these commands from the repository root (`npm --prefix ...` works on Windows/macOS/Linux shells):

1) Install frontend dependencies

```bash
npm --prefix frontend install
```

Expected output includes:

- `added ... packages` (first run) or `up to date` (subsequent runs)
- `found 0 vulnerabilities` (or npm audit summary)

2) Build frontend production bundle

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run build
```

Expected output includes:

- `vite build`
- `built in ...`

3) Run unit/component/integration suites separately

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:unit
npm --prefix frontend run test:component
npm --prefix frontend run test:integration
```

Expected output includes:

- `ok` summary for node unit tests
- `Test Files ... passed` for vitest component/integration runs

4) Run all frontend tests in one command

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:frontend
```

Expected output includes all three suites passing in sequence.

5) Install Playwright browser prerequisite (required once per machine/CI image)

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e:setup
```

This runs `playwright install chromium` and output includes browser download/install completion (or already-installed confirmation).

6) Run E2E suite

```bash
npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e
```

Expected output includes Playwright passing summary for all specs.

Frontend verification coverage includes:

- blank login form defaults unless explicitly enabled for non-production development
- offline queue sanitization (no token/header persistence)
- user-scoped API cache behavior and logout/user-switch purge hooks
- role/tab access checks (allowed + forbidden)
- search loading/empty/error/sort/pagination behavior
- restricted panel render guard denial behavior
- route-builder validation and itinerary generation flow states
- program/staffing failure and retry behavior

Playwright scenarios now verify:

- user A -> logout -> user B cross-user isolation
- restricted panel denial on unauthorized hash access attempts
- happy paths for search, graph publish, route itinerary generation, program scheduling, and staffing approval

## 4) Prompt Capability Mapping (Theme Fidelity)

The implementation maps directly to the business prompt scope:

- **Collection discovery/search**: catalog search, fuzzy matching, autocomplete, hot keywords, curation CRUD, and UI controls for category/tags/period/series filters with `pageSize <= 50`.
- **Knowledge graph curation**: draft nodes/edges, validation issues, publish with action-bound step-up.
- **Exhibit hierarchy + routes**: venue -> hall -> zone -> display case hierarchy plus visual Route Builder canvas with typed route edges (`REQUIRED_NEXT`, `OPTIONAL_BRANCH`, `ACCESSIBILITY_DETOUR`), and guided read-only navigation/itinerary consumption for ROUTE_READ roles.
- **Program operations**: scheduling with coach availability enforcement, capacity/waitlist promotion, cancellation/no-show impacts, credits, inbox notifications, printable payloads.
- **Staffing governance**: job draft/submit/approve/reject/takedown/appeal/decision workflow with ownership checks.
- **Auth and controls**: password policy, lockout accounting on repeated failures (including weak-format attempts for existing users), session security, RBAC, one-time action-bound step-up, immutable audit events.
- **Analytics/reporting**: metrics, dashboards, anomaly rules with dedupe, report runs, reconciliation artifacts with checksum sidecars.
- **Exports and masking**: permissioned exports with sensitive-field masking policies and object-level read guard on export job details.
- **Offline-ready UX**: app-shell service-worker caching + write queueing on true network failures + sync controls (API reads are network-only to avoid private cache leakage).

## 5) Scope & Non-goals

- This project is a working implementation of the museum operations prompt, not a mock-only shell.
- Some UI surfaces prioritize operational clarity over advanced visual polish.
- Current visual Route Builder uses an SVG graph editor (click-to-link + typed segment editor) rather than a third-party diagram framework; this still satisfies the required visual orchestration behavior.
- No external SaaS dependencies are required for local verification beyond MongoDB.

## 6) Frontend Visual Route Builder Verification

1) Sign in as `admin.dev` (or a role with route permissions) in the Operations Console.
2) Open **Route Builder** and create venue/hall/zone/route.
3) Add display cases and confirm they appear as nodes on the canvas and in the hierarchy tree.
4) Link nodes and assign segment type/dwell/distance; confirm typed directional edges and order labels render on canvas.
5) Commit segments and generate itinerary; confirm itinerary ordering reflects the visual route.

## 6.1) Guided Navigation Verification (ROUTE_READ)

1) Sign in as a non-manager read role (for example `reviewer.dev`).
2) Open **Guided Navigation** tab and enter a known route id.
3) Confirm route metadata and segment list load via read-only endpoints.
4) Confirm printable itinerary payloads are visible when itineraries exist.

## 7) Seeded Development Users

- `admin.dev` / `AdminSecure!2026`
- `curator.dev` / `CuratorSecure!2026`
- `reviewer.dev` / `ReviewerSecure!2026`
- `coordinator.dev` / `CoordinatorSecure!2026`
- `employer.dev` / `EmployerSecure!2026`
- `auditor.dev` / `AuditorSecure!2026`

## 8) Clean delivery packaging note

For source-of-truth delivery snapshots, exclude generated/vendor artifacts:

- `**/node_modules/`
- `frontend/dist/`

These are intentionally ignored in `.gitignore` and should not be included in submission bundles.

Verify clean packaging before delivery:

```bash
bash scripts/verify-clean-delivery.sh
```

## 9) Acceptance Evidence

| Command | Expected result | Criterion proved |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `node -e "...mongoose.connect..."` | prints `mongo-ok` | 1.1.1 external prerequisite is explicit/verifiable |
| `npm --prefix backend run start` then `curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health` | health returns `data.status` + `data.db.ready` | 1.1.1 runtime starts and health is inspectable |
| `bash scripts/smoke-local.sh` | prints `[smoke] PASS: health ok, db ready, auth login ok, catalog search ok` | 1.1.2 deterministic local verification |
| `./run_tests.sh` | summary shows `Overall : PASS` | 1.1.3 reproducible test-backed verification |
| Open Route Builder UI and follow section 6 | visual node/edge orchestration for typed segments | 1.3.1 prompt theme fidelity (visual route orchestration) |
| Review section 4 capability mapping | end-to-end feature map aligns with museum prompt | 1.3.2 no severe scope/theme deviation |

## 10) Acceptance Remediation

- Fixed pagination contract mismatch to prompt requirement (`pageSize <= 50`) across backend validation, API tests, and UI hint text.
- Hardened verification workflow by adding Mongo preflight diagnostics in `run_tests.sh` so environment failures are explicit and actionable.
- Closed security coverage gaps with object-level export read scoping tests and logger redaction tests.
- Added focused prompt-nuance coverage (12-hour late-cancel boundary, audit date filter validation, step-up expiry timeout, itinerary formula assertion).

4) Link nodes and assign segment type/dwell/distance; confirm typed directional edges and order labels render on canvas.
5) Commit segments and generate itinerary; confirm itinerary ordering reflects the visual route.

## 6.1) Guided Navigation Verification (ROUTE_READ)

1) Sign in as a non-manager read role (for example `reviewer.dev`).
2) Open **Guided Navigation** tab and enter a known route id.
3) Confirm route metadata and segment list load via read-only endpoints.
4) Confirm printable itinerary payloads are visible when itineraries exist.

## 7) Seeded Development Users

- `admin.dev` / `AdminSecure!2026`
- `curator.dev` / `CuratorSecure!2026`
- `reviewer.dev` / `ReviewerSecure!2026`
- `coordinator.dev` / `CoordinatorSecure!2026`
- `employer.dev` / `EmployerSecure!2026`
- `auditor.dev` / `AuditorSecure!2026`

## 8) Clean delivery packaging note

For source-of-truth delivery snapshots, exclude generated/vendor artifacts:

- `**/node_modules/`
- `frontend/dist/`

These are intentionally ignored in `.gitignore` and should not be included in submission bundles.

Verify clean packaging before delivery:

```bash
bash scripts/verify-clean-delivery.sh
```

## 9) Acceptance Evidence

| Command | Expected result | Criterion proved |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `node -e "...mongoose.connect..."` | prints `mongo-ok` | 1.1.1 external prerequisite is explicit/verifiable |
| `npm --prefix backend run start` then `curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health` | health returns `data.status` + `data.db.ready` | 1.1.1 runtime starts and health is inspectable |
| `bash scripts/smoke-local.sh` | prints `[smoke] PASS: health ok, db ready, auth login ok, catalog search ok` | 1.1.2 deterministic local verification |
| `./run_tests.sh` | summary shows `Overall : PASS` | 1.1.3 reproducible test-backed verification |
| Open Route Builder UI and follow section 6 | visual node/edge orchestration for typed segments | 1.3.1 prompt theme fidelity (visual route orchestration) |
| Review section 4 capability mapping | end-to-end feature map aligns with museum prompt | 1.3.2 no severe scope/theme deviation |

## 10) Acceptance Remediation

- Fixed pagination contract mismatch to prompt requirement (`pageSize <= 50`) across backend validation, API tests, and UI hint text.
- Productized frontend operations UX by replacing primary JSON-dump panels with step-based forms, status summaries, and structured tables for analytics, exports, inbox, and audit workflows.
- Hardened verification workflow by adding Mongo preflight diagnostics in `run_tests.sh` so environment failures are explicit and actionable.
- Closed security coverage gaps with object-level export read scoping tests and logger redaction tests.
- Added focused prompt-nuance coverage (12-hour late-cancel boundary, audit date filter validation, step-up expiry timeout, itinerary formula assertion).

metadata.description: Shows modified and added files after remediation

state.title: Shows modified and added files after remediation

parts.text:
Implemented. I remediated the two acceptance blockers (pagination contract + demo-like UX) in-place.

**1) What Was Changed (by file/path)**

- `backend/src/routes/catalog.js`
  - Enforced catalog max page size as **50** via `MAX_CATALOG_PAGE_SIZE`.
  - Validation now rejects `pageSize > 50`.
  - Updated validation detail message to: `page must be >=1 and pageSize must be <= 50`.

- `backend/src/docs/openapi.yaml`
  - Updated `/catalog/search` `pageSize` schema max from `51` to `50`.

- `frontend/src/constants/pagination.js` (new)
  - Added shared frontend constant: `CATALOG_MAX_PAGE_SIZE = 50`.

- `frontend/src/validators/forms.js`
  - Default clamp max changed from `51` to `50`.

- `frontend/src/lib/search-query.js`
  - Uses shared `CATALOG_MAX_PAGE_SIZE` for query clamping.

- `frontend/src/components/SearchDiscoveryTab.jsx`
  - Updated UI hint to `page size (max 50)`.
  - Added inline validation for invalid page size:
    - blocks non-integer / <1
    - blocks `>50` with clear message (`Page size cannot exceed 50.`)
  - Retained clamping behavior in query builder for safe handling.

- `frontend/src/App.jsx`
  - Productized analytics/exports/inbox/audit panels:
    - replaced JSON-dump-first UX with step-based, user-driven workflows and structured tables/cards.
    - analytics now split into explicit steps:
      1) save metric/rule
      2) create dashboard
      3) save report definition + run report
    - exports now split into:
      1) request export form
      2) refresh job status + load artifacts
    - inbox now uses filterable table + explicit select/read/print flow.
    - audit now uses filter fields + table output + pagination summary.
  - Added status badge helpers, datetime formatting, and metadata summarization.
  - Kept existing auth/RBAC/step-up/guard behavior intact.

- `frontend/src/styles.css`
  - Added styles for structured product UX:
    - `summary-grid`, `summary-card`
    - `status-badge` + success/error/pending variants.

- `API_tests/api.integration.test.js`
  - Boundary contract updated:
    - `pageSize=50` => expect `200`
    - `pageSize=51` => expect `400`
  - Added assertion for clear validation detail mentioning `<= 50`.

- `frontend/tests/unit/query-and-validation.test.js`
  - Updated pagination expectations from 51 to 50.

- `unit_tests/frontend-search-query.test.js`
  - Updated clamp expectation from 51 to 50.

- `frontend/tests/component/search-discovery.test.jsx`
  - Added UI boundary test:
    - `50` accepted and sent
    - `51` blocked with clear inline error and no request fired.

- `frontend/tests/integration/operations-console.integration.test.jsx` (new)
  - Added coverage for structured step-based analytics/exports/inbox/audit UX.
  - Verifies panel flow is usable without relying on JSON dump panels.

- `frontend/tests/integration/program-staffing.integration.test.jsx`
- `frontend/tests/integration/route-builder.integration.test.jsx`
  - Increased per-test timeout to reduce flaky false failures under heavier test load.

- `README.md`
  - Updated all catalog pagination references to **50 max / 51 rejected**.
  - Added acceptance remediation note describing productized frontend UX change and rationale.

---

**2) Why These Changes Fix Each Acceptance Blocker**

- **Blocker A: Pagination contract mismatch (50 vs 51)**
  - Fixed consistently across:
    - backend validation/handler (`catalog.js`)
    - OpenAPI docs (`openapi.yaml`)
    - frontend validation/clamping/hints (`SearchDiscoveryTab`, `search-query`, `validators`, shared constant)
    - tests (unit + API + UI component)
    - README documentation
  - Required boundary behavior now implemented:
    - `pageSize=50` accepted
    - `pageSize=51` rejected with clear validation message.

- **Blocker B: Demo-like frontend UX**
  - Replaced primary JSON-dump style UX in critical ops tabs (analytics/exports/inbox/audit) with:
    - step-based user flows
    - structured tables/cards
    - status badges
    - explicit loading/submitting feedback
    - filterable/readable operational views
  - This aligns UI with backend capabilities and removes “one-click scripted demo” feel.

- **Security / permissions preserved**
  - No weakening of existing auth, RBAC, step-up, or feature guards.
  - Existing forbidden-state/route-guard behavior remains in place.

---

**3) Test Commands Run + Concise Results**

- `cd frontend && npm run build`  
  - ✅ Pass (build successful)

- `cd frontend && npm run test:frontend`  
  - ✅ Pass  
  - Unit: 12 passed  
  - Component: 6 passed  
  - Integration: 4 passed

- `cd frontend && npm run test:e2e`  
  - ✅ Pass (7/7 Playwright tests passed)

- `node --test unit_tests/frontend-search-query.test.js`  
  - ✅ Pass (1/1)

- `node --check API_tests/api.integration.test.js`  
  - ✅ Syntax check pass

---

**4) Remaining Risks / Follow-ups**

- The updated API boundary assertions in `API_tests/api.integration.test.js` were updated and syntax-checked, but I did **not** run the full API integration suite against a live backend+Mongo instance in this pass.
- Non-blocking existing warning remains during frontend build: Tailwind `content` config warning. It does not break build/tests but should be cleaned up separately.
